_comment: Bridge config for handbook-coverage.test.js. `aliases` maps a Handbook machineField (as written in handboek/data.ts) to the field that actually carries it today on the rendered Agent Card (assets/agents-data.js) or the manifest schema. An alias means 'same meaning, different name/shape' = drift that is mapped but should be unified (see RECONCILIATION-MEMO.md). A machineField that is neither a direct match nor aliased is UNRESOLVED = the norm points at a field nobody implemented. Once the canonical schema from the memo lands, aliases should shrink to zero.
_unmappable_comment: The entries below are INTENTIONALLY left unmapped so the test reports them as UNRESOLVED. Each is a governed control the Handbook relies on but the current card/schema does not implement. This list is the FORWARD failure set the memo's canonical schema must close. Do not add fake aliases to silence them.
_enforcement_comment: Fields the RUNTIME uses to block or allow an agent call (Boek V Art. 5.9 / Boek VIII Art. 8.30: 'a call missing a required oversight moment is blocked'). Each must be named by at least one article, else the platform enforces a control no norm authorises. Listed here in the vocabulary the CURRENT card actually carries — that is what the runtime reads today — so the REVERSE check surfaces where enforcement runs ahead of the norm.

enforcementFields.note: runtime tier gate; Handbook names this bna.riskScoring.inhuurTier (Art. 4.5, 8.11)
geldig/gebroken gate for autonomous use; Handbook expresses this via bna.riskScoring.suspended (Art. 8.27) + cardHash (Art. 6.3)
EU AI Act Annex III flag driving high-risk treatment (Art. 4.1, 8.4)
v2.1 pin-match gate: POST /v1/connectors/{id}/invoke step 3 refuses on pin break; the Handbook has no connector article governing this (memo §2.4, migration step 6)
